topical_tags.term: Cheresh (deaf-mute; deaf or mute)
Re'iyah (appearing at the pilgrimage; olat re'iyah as korban)
Hakhel (assembly; reading of Torah every seven years)
Simcha (joy; shalmei simcha as korban of thanksgiving)
Bar Chiyuva (one obligated in mitzvot; subject to obligation)
Blind in one eye; physical defects and exemptions
Lema'an Yishme'u (in order to hear)
Lema'an Yilmedu (in order to learn; in order to teach)
Gezerah Shavah (verbal analogy; hermeneutical rule)
Shalosh Regalim (three pilgrimage festivals)
Pa'amim (feet; footsteps; occasions)
Baalei Kabin (those with prosthetics; wooden leg wearers)
Oleh L'regel (going up to the pilgrimage)
Gerim (converts; those who accept the yoke of mitzvot)
Nedivei Amim (generous ones of the nations; converts gathering)
Rabbi Yochanan ben Zakkai (early amora; teacher of Rabbi Eliezer)
Rabbi Eliezer (stringent amora; upholder of mesora/tradition)
Rabbi Yehoshua (lenient amora; accepts multiple voices and votes)
Rabbi Eleazar ben Azariah (amora; led beit midrash for one week in rotation)
Rabbi Yochanan ben Broka and Rabbi Eleazar Chasma (students visiting Rabbi Yehoshua)
Rabbi Yose ben Durmaski (offers novel teaching without deference; provokes Rabbi Eliezer)
Pki'in (location where Rabbi Yehoshua lived)
Chidush (novel interpretation; innovation in Torah learning)
Mesora (tradition; received teaching; halacha l'Moshe mi-Sinai
Divrei Chachamim Kadorbanot (words of sages like goads and nails)
Parah V'Rabah (fruitful and multiply; Torah spreading and bearing fruit)
Ba'alei Asufot (study groups; masters of assemblies)
Sod Hashem Liyereiav (God's secret is to those who fear Him; secret tradition)
Ammon and Moav (Transjordan lands; subject of halakhic dispute about tithes and shemita)
Maaser Ani (poor tithe; given in place of second tithe outside Israel)
Shemita (sabbatical year; land lies fallow)
Halacha L'Moshe mi-Sinai (law to Moses from Sinai; oldest tradition)
One Shepherd (all Torah voices unified; all from God)
